goal: Read through the directory structure of the codebase to identify inefficiencies or gaps that aren't immediately obvious from a high-level overview.

plan_steps.description: List all files in the codebase directory.
acceptance_criteria: A comprehensive list of all files in the codebase is generated.

plan_steps.description: Search for specific patterns or keywords that indicate inefficiencies or gaps.
acceptance_criteria: Patterns like 'TODO', 'FIXME', 'XXX' are identified in the codebase files.
Common inefficient coding practices (e.g., redundant loops, unused variables) are flagged.

plan_steps.description: Analyze file structure and dependencies to identify potential inefficiencies or gaps.
acceptance_criteria: A dependency graph of the codebase is generated.
Modules with high coupling or low cohesion are identified.

plan_steps.description: Review and document identified inefficiencies or gaps in a report.
acceptance_criteria: A detailed report outlining identified inefficiencies and gaps is created.
The report includes recommendations for improvement.

spoken_summary: I wasn't able to complete that scan — I ran into a tool limitation and couldn't dig into the directory structure. Do you want to point me to a specific folder to explore, or should we try a different approach?
